def get_sections(tex_source):
    parts = tex_source.split("\\section")
    titles = []
    for p in parts[1:]:
        title = p.split("{")[1].split("}")[0]
        titles.append(title)
    return titles

An HTML document forms a rooted tree where each element is a node. Depth-first traversal visits all n nodes in O(n) time. The maximum tree depth h satisfies h <= n - 1 for any rooted tree structure.
An XML document forms a rooted tree T = (V, E) where |E| = |V| - 1. Each element is a vertex, and parent-child relationships define edges. The tree can be traversed in linear time O(|V|).

An unordered list with n items renders in O(n) time. If each item contains at most m characters, total rendering takes O(nm) operations. Adding a list element increases the DOM tree depth by exactly two.

HTML tag matching follows a stack discipline: for every opening tag there must be a closing tag in LIFO order, equivalent to balanced parentheses. This can be verified in O(n) time using a single stack.

LaTeX hierarchical numbering uses section i.j.k to denote the k-th subsection of section j in chapter i. The maximum possible distinct section numbers equals the product of allowed values at each level.

The first website, info.cern.ch, was created by Tim Berners-Lee in 1991 at CERN in Switzerland. It described the World Wide Web project and explained how to create web pages using the new HTML format.

Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web and was knighted in 2004 by Queen Elizabeth II. He originally proposed HTML as a way for physicists at CERN to share research documents across computer networks.
The average web page in 2024 is about 2.5 megabytes in size, roughly 100 times larger than the average page in the year 2003. Most of this increase comes from images and JavaScript frameworks.

XML was derived from SGML, first standardized in 1986. Its name stands for Extensible Markup Language. A W3C working group designed it to be simultaneously human-readable and machine-parseable for data exchange.
The longest HTML element name in the specification is 'blockquote' at ten characters. By contrast, the shortest are 'a', 'b', 'i', 'p', 'q', 's', and 'u', each being just one character.
